options.text: RAM is volatile, ROM is non-volatile

questions.subject: Computer

questions.text: Observation method consist of which observer methods?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Participant observer is a research method where the researcher becomes involved in the activity or group they are studying. This can provide rich and detailed data, but it can also be difficult to remain objective.
Non-participant observer is a research method where the researcher observes the activity or group from a distance, without participating themselves.

Direct observe is not a distinct observer method on its own. Observation itself can be direct, where the researcher observes the behavior or phenomenon firsthand, or indirect, where the researcher collects data through other means like interviews or surveys.

questions.text: In SDLC analysis report consist of the following components?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Understanding the current system is crucial for identifying areas for improvement and ensuring the new system integrates effectively.

options.text: Explain the problems in the existing system
options.explanation: Identifying problems helps define the goals for the new system and avoid replicating shortcomings.

options.text: Describe the requirements for new system & make recommendations for future
options.explanation: A clear vision for the new system and its future development is essential for a successful project.

The correct answer is (d) All of these..A Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) analysis report is a comprehensive document that outlines the current state of a system and identifies opportunities for improvement. It should include the following components:Explanation of how the current system works: This section provides a detailed overview of the existing system, including its functionalities, processes, and limitations.Identification of problems in the existing system: This section focuses on the shortcomings of the current system. It should identify areas where the system is inefficient, ineffective, or error-prone.Description of the requirements for a new system: This section outlines the needs and expectations for the new system. It should address how the new system will address the shortcomings identified in the existing system.Recommendations for the future: This section may include suggestions for future enhancements or upgrades to the new system. It can also address broader considerations such as scalability, maintainability, and security.By including all of these components, an SDLC analysis report provides a valuable foundation for the development of a new system. It ensures that the new system is designed to meet the needs of the users and address the limitations of the existing system.

questions.text: What type of organization is best suited for a project with clearly defined goals and tasks?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Functional organization groups employees by their area of expertise, such as marketing, finance, or human resources. This structure can be less efficient for projects because team members may have to report to multiple managers.

Matrix organization is a hybrid structure that combines elements of a functional organization and a projectized organization. Team members may report to both a functional manager and a project manager. This structure can be complex and can lead to confusion about who is responsible for what.

Projectized organization is a type of organizational structure that is specifically designed for projects. It has a clear hierarchy with a project manager at the top who is responsible for the overall success of the project. The team members who work on the project report to the project manager and are typically specialists in their own areas. This structure is well-suited for projects with clearly defined goals and tasks because it allows for clear decision-making and accountability.
Team organization is a broad term that can refer to any group of people who work together towards a common goal. It is not a specific type of organizational structure

questions.text: What is the term for adjusting and improving a system to meet new user needs and technology requirements?
questions.subject: Computer

options.explanation: Preventative maintenance is the practice of performing routine maintenance on a system to prevent problems from arising. While preventative maintenance is important for keeping a system running smoothly, it is not the same as enhancement , which is about adding new features.
Update can refer to installing a new version of software or fixing bugs in an existing system. An update may or may not be an enhancement.
Enhancement is the most fitting term. It refers to the process of adding new features or functionalities to an existing system to improve its capabilities and meet evolving needs.

Implementation refers to the process of putting a plan or system into effect. While implementation may be necessary to carry out enhancements , it is not the same thing as the overall process of improvement.

questions.explanation: Enhancement is the most fitting term. It refers to the process of adding new features or functionalities to an existing system to improve its capabilities and meet evolving needs.
